mnemonics: Subagging: 'SUB' (subset) + 'AGG' (aggregate) to remember it's about aggregating models on subsets
keyPoints: Subagging combines bagging and subsampling without replacement
It reduces variance like bagging but uses less data per model
Works well when data is abundant and models are complex
commonMistakes: Confusing subagging with traditional bagging (which uses bootstrapping)
Assuming subagging always performs better than bagging (depends on data characteristics)
Forgetting that subagging models are trained on disjoint subsets
